verses.1: But as for you, proclaim the things which are fitting for sound doctrine.
verses.2: Older men are to be temperate, dignified, self-controlled, sound in faith, in love, in perseverance.
verses.3: Older women likewise are to be reverent in their behavior, not malicious gossips nor enslaved to much wine, teaching what is good,
verses.4: so that they may encourage the young women to love their husbands, to love their children,
verses.5: to be sensible, pure, workers at home, kind, being subject to their own husbands, so that the word of God will not be dishonored.
verses.6: Likewise urge the young men to be sensible;
verses.7: in all things show yourself to be an example of good deeds, with purity in doctrine, dignified,
verses.8: sound in speech which is beyond reproach, so that the opponent will be put to shame, having nothing bad to say about us.
verses.9: Urge slaves to be subject to their own masters in everything, to be pleasing, not argumentative,
verses.10: not stealing, but showing all good faith so that they will adorn the doctrine of God our Savior in every respect.
verses.11: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all people,
verses.12: instructing us to deny ungodliness and worldly desires and to live sensibly, righteously, and in a godly manner in the present age,
verses.13: looking for the blessed hope and the appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior, Christ Jesus,
verses.14: who gave Himself for us to redeem us from every lawless deed, and to purify for Himself a people for His own possession, eager for good deeds.
verses.15: These things speak and exhort, and rebuke with all authority. No one is to disregard you.
